review_text:
My husband and I recently stayed here for 3 nights with our caravan..
We were so impressed with this caravan park and the site we had .
Both Sam and Graham in reception were very friendly, helpful and welcoming.
We would highly recommend this caravan park.
Ulladulla was such a beautiful spot during our 7 week road trip so glad we stayed here.
Very nice place, good price, very clean cabins

Caravan stay close to Ulladulla park is in the middle of getting some refurbishments so a bit busy with construction vehicles and machinery. The facilities are very well maintained and clean with a good camp kitchen. Only issue was the sites are a bit tight to manoeuvre into, and our site was near the new dump point, so a lot of pedestrian traffic all day and the occasional wafting of effluent when cassettes were emptied. A great location beside the lake with easy walking along the shore and to nearby shops, staff were generally friendly and helpful with information.
Great family park.

Fantastic caravan park! We stayed in the three bed cabin with a spa, the rooms were lovely, very comfy bed and it had everything we needed. There was a minor ant problem in the kitchen we noticed on our last days.
The park itself has everything you'd want, three playgrounds, jumping pad, put put, heated pool and lots of room to ride bikes. It's attached to a lake, so you need to drive to get to the beach. We had a wonderful time!

Bit dissapointed with the one car in one car out rule.
The only time I have come across this.
Having two cars with all our set up in made it frustrating.
With three kids in tow made pack up after the rain a bit stressful.
We were locked in the park for taking in two cars to pack up for one hour.

Others had boats cars & caravan so not sure what the problem was.
The sites are tight.

Showers we nice & the location good but that's about all.

We recently stayed for three nights in one of the pet-friendly cabins with our two toy poodles. Unfortunately, as soon as we walked in, there was quite a strong unpleasant smell. Having carpet in the main living area and bedroom also seemed like an unusual choice for a pet-friendly cabin, as it likely contributes to lingering odours over time.

We also experienced flying ants coming out of the wall inside the cabin. After spraying them, they began falling down onto the lounge area, which was very unpleasant and not something we expected to deal with during our stay.

During our visit, we went into my mum and sister’s cabins (Cabin 8 and Cabin 10), and there was a noticeable difference in how well-presented and maintained they were compared to the pet-friendly cabin. Their cabins felt fresh and updated, whereas the pet-friendly cabin felt quite run-down in comparison. Considering the price was very similar, it was disappointing to feel like having pets meant staying in a much lower-quality cabin.

On a positive note, the linen and towels were fresh and clean, and aside from the smell and ant issue, the stay itself was pleasant and the park overall was enjoyable. The pet-friendly cabins would really benefit from updating and improved pest control so they match the standard of the other cabins.
One of the best holiday Parks in the area

I have been coming to this holiday park since I was a kid, stay here regularly, never had an issue until my stay this week, booked 3 days cabin was filthy, not clean at all and all the cutlery, and crockery was the same, water was turned off for repairs all day with no warning at all, couldn’t make my baby’s bottle or shower to wash my hair for an event I was in the area for, I went to office to ask when it would be back on and they said in a sec and was not in a sec it was hours after that, maintenance isn’t the issue it’s the lack of communication I could have been prepared had I have been told, I wanted to extend my stay so went to the office to ask if I could and was told there was no availability however when checking availability on the website it says that there is, other work was being done in the park below our cabin, concrete saws were going off and it was not an enjoyable stay for this reason also, website advises that there was fire extinguisher work being done in cabins and apologies for any inconvenience but failed to say about the water being turned off for the day or that there will be concrete saws going off. Also people coming in at 2:30am going through the bins opposite my cabin to collect bottles and cans and waking us up with the noise not to mention the torches they were shining through our window, oh and one more thing, due to most of our day being wasted with the no water issue and us not being allowed to go extend for whatever the reason actually was, we asked if we could check out at a later time than 10, male in the office was very unpleasant to deal with and only gave us an hour, really disappointed as I come here all the time and love it, but this time was absolutely terrible
Lovely cabin and on lake short drive to beach

Very clean amenities and well kept grounds it’s in a good location. We camped and the only downside is there was no security around the jumping pillow and most days the older kids played footy or bull rush and there were a few not so happy parents of little ones trying to have fun

Tents and caravans packed so closely it feels like you're in a refugee camp. Giant yank tanks taking up the remaining space between sites. Someone stole my last 3 beers from the communal fridge. Many people smoking and riding e-scooters although it's apparently banned
Rooms need an overhaul, wifi not so good, noisy if near highway.

Wow what a big caravan park! Very clean and tidy has 2 pools 1 deep the other for the kids .. however this is located at the top of the hill. So if you’re on the lower level and not good with walking you will have to drive up and try to find a park. Has mini golf, tennis court, jumping pillow.
Has 8 ensuites they do have 2 steps up to get inside.
We had a sight with artificial grass near the lake and jetty’s ..amazing!!
Has 2 jetties and a boat ramp.
Hugh lake!!!
They also feed the rainbow lorikeets in the afternoons ..so cute! A lot of wildlife including ducks
Down the back they have a nice camp kitchen first one they had 2 slow cookers but no air fryers. Big TV.
Toilet block very clean and nice $4 for washers and dryers has top and front loading machines and 2 dryers in the one near us.
A small play ground it’s near the bottom part of the lake also.
Has a cafe around the corner about a 5 min walk if that .. closer if yr near the front of the park .
There is also a little news agent and an express shop for yr needs.
Fish off the jetty if u like or on the below section of the bridge.
Walk over the bridge and there is a bakery and a fish and chip shop.
Staff are very friendly and helpful.
They have cabins but not sure on how many but there is quite a few!.
You are not far from Ulladulla at all.
Good first day so far 👍🙂
Beautiful spot on the lake.  Our van site was right on the lake  but most are not although are close by.  Clean and well maintained facilities. Very reasonable rates. I'd stay there again.
Amazing place to be... clean and cozy... service is excellent.

Avoid staying at weekends or school holidays if you have a dog..dog friendly sites are located in a small area right next to the bouncy pillow 🥴 okay if you have kids with you but not great listening to them all day...even started at 6.30 in the morning. Overall feel of the camping area is a bit grim with all the old annuals. Area with the cabins had a nice feel and the pool is located there too.
Nice place to camp, either with a tent, camper or caravan. Its large, lots of amenities with the beach and coffee close by.
Good customer service and lots of facilities for kids to spend their time
Clean and tidy with great facilities. Caught fish right off the jetty with the kids.

Thank you for having us, Your park is fantastic! Can not faulted,  only that you put us next to the ashtray, and could not open the window! Everyone get there to smoke! Smokers should have a side like the dogs, so people like me who is non a smoker can breath clean air, but thank you, like the park.
Stayed in cabin 4 and it was terrific. Excellent amenities, even a washing machine with detergent. Clean, well appointed, great view. Would definitely recommend.
Riverside caravan site are great

Allways an amazing place love the actives allways so cheap the only thing i was a little dissapointed about was the pool was clean and all but oh boy was it cold overall recomed staying here great place
Cockies the birds all amazing
But the games room was very bad rubbish ever where and they toke away the table tennis
I am literally a stones throw from the Princes Highway. And I don't have a good arm. How did no review mention the TRAFFIC NOISE?? I have paid $100 for a cabin in hell.
Beautiful location on the lake close to shops and a lovely little bakery.the facilities are updated and exceptionally clean as is the whole park.have stayed here multiple times.

Hidden gem, 5 minutes south of Ulladulla. Fantastic customer service, plenty of cabins and caravan sites. Well maintained grounds. Clean and updated amenities. Located on the shores of a lake, a couple of docks. Playground for children. Bird feeding. Short walk to local shops.

As a pet owner we were only allowed to have our caravan right next to the jumping pillow near the entrance.
Lovely location but we won't return here

Great place to stay, cabins were comfortable and clean.
Self-contained so you could cook or drive to the nearest restaurant.
Friendly. I'm looking forward to going there again.

Good localtion,  a bit dated cabins that were clean and recently painted.

Very well maintained park, and friendly staff.

A great place launch the kayak for a day of fishing
Dog friendly cabins makes it my pick. There are others I may not have tried, but don't change what isn't broken, right? 😁

Great park
Very well organised
Great location on the water
Good facilities and playground
Couldnt get internet to work but that no big deal
Camp kitchens looked very good with a large fridge
The only way you can
Good location and facilities
It was a wonderful holiday park 😀

Pre booked 2 night stay March, powered lakeside site. We had site 136, large site, concrete slab easy drive in access for the van. Amenities are refurbished to a modern standard, good hot showers although the water doesn’t drain as well as it could and ends up in the dressing side. Great camp kitchen with large fridge and standard facilities. Laundry has coin operated machines and driers as well as plenty of line space.
Birdlife is plentiful as were the rabbits.
No road noise, park has easy vehicular access from the highway via the roundabout
Very popular park for fishing and water activities and worth a visit if you are in the area.
Roaches in the cabins not just one many roaches
Only stayed 1 night but the cabins were amazing, clean and very large

Just arrived looks great 👍
Looking forward to seeing the sights

It's a fantastic location to get away with the kids, with convenient spots for fishing of the jetty. The resort is a short drive, or a cycle ride away, from local beaches.
There is a local supermarket and a few cafes next to the resort, with both serving good coffee.
Memories from here when it was just a caravan park and open air picture theatre were great for a revisit
Good location, a little pricy for the facilities offered and comparable places. Nice enough though

This was a great location only a couple of minutes away from Ulladulla clubs fast food and beaches with the lake at your doorstep
Service was lovely cabins neat and tidy with everything you need
Our large cabin could sleep up to 8 beds were comfy and we were so close to pools and BBQ area
Pool was beautiful and clean
Definitely will stay again

Best park ever! We just had a great 2 days,the kids fished non stop off the jetty as they always do,we’ve always stayed in cabins but with a jayco flamingo now down by the water is awesome,great amenities,great staff and a beautiful spot

Unfortunately, I've had to change my review! This WAS the best park in the Ulladulla region, but the change over has flaws! We hadn't stayed since the change over and decided we'd give it another go. We booked through booking.com, and when I called to confirm payment they lady was rude (thanks kim) she first off told me they didn't have any disability access cabins and when I had advised her we had stayed with the park plenty of times she said let me check ill put you on hold. Came back and said yes, the cabin has a ramp (we knew this already). we were told what cabin we were staying in, and we were excited to be close to the park and pool. On arrival, we were put into a different cabin, and this is where it went downhill. The ramp to the room had a small gap with a bump. This was a trip hazard, and instantly, my son fell on the bump and gap, we went inside to find some changes, which were nice but otherwise the kitchen utensils had mostly been removed and there was bare minimum left behind we went to cook pancakes and took the fry pan out to find someone had scratched the enamel off and the pan wasn't able to be used, cutlery was dirty & oily as well as the cups/mugs which left me having to clean them all so we could use them. The park by the pool is very outdated and dangerous. There were holes in the metal, and it was all rusted out. If a child got their finger or toe, it would be sliced very dangerous! They used to have bait for use, but the machines and fridges have been removed, and the kids' play room has very minimal. As we were leaving, we also noticed a disability access room right across from us, so this leaves me to believe the reception staff have no clue about the park facilities. Pulling back the sheets in one of the rooms had public hair on it, the 2nd room with the bunks only 2 of the beds were made. Kids didn't want to sleep on the mattress as they were thin foam mattresses. I would definitely think twice about paying through the roof for very basic accommodation!

Camped in a powered tent site for 2 nights. Good size sites with good square of artificial turf so held up well in rain. Good amenities. Camp kitchen was good and others only used to wash up but bbq was slow even by push button standards. Too wet to try mini golf but looked good. Will need to give it another go and hopefully luckier with weather.

This park advertised as being dog friendly, it only has 2 rows as you drive in the gate next to the jumping pillow that allows dogs, there reason you can’t have site near the lake as the dogs disturb the wildlife, someone forgot to tell the wildlife that as we had all sorts around our van and neither the wildlife or the dog was concerned. You could take your dog for a walk to the lake for a swim or picnic there all day. Doesn’t make sense. Apart from that the staff were extremely helpful. I won’t be back.

Absolutely can't thank the staff enough. Kids had a ball. We even extended our stay for a couple of nights. Cabin was clean and the park is well maintained.
Kids loved mini golf and the jumping pillow.
The 2 pools are heated.
Gas bbq are around the whole park and are clean and well maintained. Table seating and park areas all across the park.
Happy Parents and Happy kids

Fantastic location with plenty to do. But Management seem unable or not willing to control bad language or noise after 10pm. With 2 night of doof doof music we finally got to sleep at 11:30 the first night and 12:30 the second night. I like music but 5 hours or more of the same load sound would drive anyone crazy. It seems a joke that you sign a code of conduct when you arrive to find it ineffective and not inforced
Stunning lakeside caravan park. Amenities up to date and clean. Kids play areas. Fishing, boat ramp, dog friendly.

Loved our stay! Alot of things to do and places to drive to and visit closeby. Generally clean but have to help yourself with getting your towels replaced. They do not change the towels everyday or maybe there is a need to go to reception to ask for fresh ones. Anyway, we had lots of fun considering that we came in as a big group and we almost had the entire bbq area to ourselves.

If you like not being bothered by staff and have little to no interactions with one, then this is the place to go. Easy in and out and gives the sense of having your own holiday home with in the southcoast area.
Great family place awesome for fishing very clean and tidy cabins are amazing great family place lot's to do everything you wish for, it's all there great location too
A very nice relaxing quite location

I go to Burrill Lake every year and it is incredible especially if you have a boat sometimes I can do out side of the place and then I boat to the beach that is very fun plus the game room. There is a glitch with a chocolate machine. put a two dollar coin into the slot, and then the coin comes out but you still get a turn
We have been there twice the cabins are nice and cosie love the lake view the beds are great it's going to be our new holiday getaway place
Relaxing and quiet.
Beautiful spot by the lake. Good service & facilities  Lots of wildlife.
Great spacious clean cabin with a good view of Burrill Lake 😀

Location and the view was simply brilliant.the amenities were new and clean. The staff were very helpful. Its a short walk away from an IGA /cafe and a nice stroll to the beach.the campground was well lit and even with astro turf where indicated.
Only reduced 1 star on the room, as the campsite, we were allocated was odd shaped, small and marked in a way that sort of caged us in when the next site was occupied.
Usually the other campers are understanding but if you encountered a real estate moghul who wanted to use every inch of their camp borders lines, like we did, then the site is good for a small tent only. While this isnt the campgrounds fault, marking the sites equally would have helped or atleast an indication while booking this site that it was smaller than the rest.
Otherwise we loved the stay and will definately be coming back to this campground.
Great spot thanks

The lady at reception was very unhappy and portrayed that onto her costomers(us) as she was very unhelpful when we made inquiries, or contacted the office numerous times or even checked out. Maybe some training or a holidays might help her like her job a bit better.
Camping gelegen aan de oevers van het Burril Lake. Goede uitvalsbasis voor tripjes langs de kust voor o.a. dolfijnspotten.
Beautiful staff and location
Just spent 6 nights on a caravan site. Great site our van is 25ft no problem plenty of room. Great Park great staff great location. Would definitely stay again.
Dynamic pricing, what a load of rubbish, basically you are saying we will rip you off if we can and when it's quiet we will reduce prices, this should be illegal, blatant gouging.

I rate the rooms 3 because we only use a caravan site and the amenities have been bad for many years although being renovated now. I rate service 4 because the staff have always been great. I rate location 4 because it is a great spot.
However, I have cancelled my normal 6 week stay due to the ridiculous increase in rates. Over 12 years the rate has gone from $28 a night for long stay to the current $60 a night. Last year we paid $43. Dynamic pricing they call it. The busier it is the more they charge! Will not be back at those rates charged for a long time visitor twice a year and a member of Discovery. Very poor attitude to your members and it will backfire eventually.

Great place for a family getaway, cabins are nice and roomy, plenty of privacy, heaps of things for the kids to do. Backs on to the lake with fishing wharves. Plenty of cafes and shops nearby. Games rooms, jumping balloon and a heated pool. I was very impressed with the facility for the price. Great value holiday.

Stayed for two nights in the cabins. Really nice park, has a small pool and three playgrounds, plus mini golf which is free for guests. Great location for the lake. The team at the reception were so friendly.
Best place for camping.
La cabin esta muy bien. Camas muy comodas, mucho espacio y muy confortable
Fantastic location with great views and access to the lake.

Hi im Jody higgins
We booked 4 night in a powered cvan site opposite the gorgeous lake needless to say the view was beautiful the only issue was you have to park your car across the front of the site which blocks most of the view i had paid to constanly see, the park was great, the shower & toilet blocks were super clean, camp kitchen was awsome everything you need it even had a pizza oven, w/machines & dryers avail, mimi golf, kids park & jumping pillow, lovely pool & baby pool, theres lorakeets, duck & other birds that visit your site & lorakeet feeding, staff were lovely, local shop 2min walk, we really enjoyed our stay Thanks discovery park

Great place to stay.

Office lady is very rude

Bed very clean and comfortable
Bits of mould on bathroom ceiling and toilet cistern. Ceiling fans quite dirty. Ceiling and rail on back deck full of spider webs spiders and eggs.bird droppings on deck not pleasant.
Other than theses things we enjoyed our stay.
Park is well maintained, facilities clean, well supplied with camp kitchens, barbecues, shower/ toilets.  It is very convenient for launching kayaks onto a pristine, sheltered bay.
Great Campground near the lake, clean facilities and lots of budgies appear in the afternoon and it's great for photos

We loved this park , what a hidden gem.
Just far enough out of town and amazing area.
We'll be back again !!!!!!
Pleasant accommodation on the NSW coast. Room was clean and tidy. Kids thoroughly enjoyed the pool, jumping pillow, fishing, possum and putt putt golf. Close to Burril Lake and local shops.
Good place

First time here.  Great site opposite camp kitchen and loos.  (Site 173).  Amenities seem to have been recently renovated,  showers are great, shower head faces sideways not at the door, dividing wall between shower and dressing area, GREAT.  On site food van for breaky (egg & bacon roll).   Staff friendly and helpful.  They text daily "what's on" every day.  HIGHLY RECOMMEND.   Would definately stay again.
